---
title: "Shadow Tealbook A"
subtitle: "Current Economic and Financial Conditions"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import os, sys, json, re, warnings
import urllib.request
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.subplots as sp
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path('.').resolve().parent))
from data.fred_pulls import pull_series, get_series

# ── Global color & line system ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# C1 = Black     = primary series / actuals        (solid)
# C2 = Fed red   = secondary series / forecasts    (dash)
# C3 = Gray      = alternative / reference lines   (dot)
# C4 = Green     = used ONLY for PCE growth chart
# C5 = Orange    = volatility indicators (VIX, claims)
C1 = '#000000'; C2 = '#C41E3A'; C3 = '#6B6B6B'; C4 = '#2E7D32'; C5 = '#E65100'
BLACK = C1; RED = C2; GRAY = C3; GREEN = C4; ORANGE = C5  # aliases

def line(color, style=None, width=None):
    """Consistent line style helper — always use this, never hardcode.
    Defaults: C1/C2=solid, C3=dash (reference lines), C5=solid."""
    if style is None:
        style = 'dash' if color == C3 else 'solid'
    w = width if width else (2.0 if style == 'solid' else 1.5)
    return dict(color=color, width=w, dash=style)

# ── Chart windows ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TODAY           = datetime.today()
CHART_START     = (TODAY - timedelta(days=6*365)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
CHART_START_GDP = '2015-01-01'
DATA_START      = '2010-01-01'
now_str         = TODAY.strftime('%B %d, %Y')

# ── Date helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def quarter_label(dt):
    q = (dt.month - 1) // 3 + 1
    return f"Q{q} {dt.year}"

def month_label(dt):
    return dt.strftime('%B %Y')

def latest_label(series, freq='monthly'):
    dt = pd.Timestamp(series.index[-1])
    return quarter_label(dt) if freq == 'quarterly' else month_label(dt)

# ── Auto-counters ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_tbl_count = 0
_fig_count = 0

def tbl_title(name):
    global _tbl_count
    _tbl_count += 1
    return (f'<p style="color:{C1};font-weight:bold;font-size:0.95rem;'
            f'margin:1.4rem 0 0.3rem 0;">Table {_tbl_count} — {name}</p>')

def next_fig():
    global _fig_count
    _fig_count += 1
    return _fig_count

# ── Plotly layout helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def fed_layout(title='', height=420, showlegend=True):
    return dict(
        title=dict(text=title, font=dict(size=13, color='#222')),
        height=height, plot_bgcolor='white', paper_bgcolor='white',
        font=dict(family='Source Sans Pro, Arial, sans-serif', size=11),
        legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='bottom', y=1.02,
                    xanchor='left', x=0, font=dict(size=10)),
        showlegend=showlegend,
        margin=dict(l=50, r=30, t=60, b=40),
        xaxis=dict(showgrid=False, zeroline=False, linecolor='#ccc',
                   range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')]),
        yaxis=dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor='#f0f0f0', zeroline=False),
        hovermode='x unified',
    )

def xaxis_cfg(start=CHART_START_GDP):
    today = pd.Timestamp.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
    return dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor='#eeeeee', zeroline=False,
                tickfont=dict(size=9), dtick='M24', tickformat='%Y',
                type='date', range=[start, today])

def base_layout(title, subtitle=None, start=CHART_START_GDP, r_margin=15):
    full_title = (f'<b style="font-size:11px">{title}</b>'
                  + (f'<br><span style="font-size:10px;color:#666">{subtitle}</span>'
                     if subtitle else ''))
    return dict(
        title=dict(text=full_title, x=0.5, xanchor='center', yanchor='top',
                   font=dict(size=11, color=C1), pad=dict(b=8)),
        width=420, height=310,
        margin=dict(l=55, r=r_margin, t=65, b=50),
        paper_bgcolor='white', plot_bgcolor='#fafafa',
        xaxis=xaxis_cfg(start),
        yaxis=dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor='#eeeeee', zeroline=False,
                   tickfont=dict(size=9), autorange=True),
        legend=dict(orientation='h', yanchor='top', y=-0.18,
                    xanchor='center', x=0.5, font=dict(size=9),
                    bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)'),
        font=dict(family='Arial, sans-serif', size=10),
        hovermode='x unified',
    )

# ── GDP figure queue (2-per-row grid) ────────────────────────────────────────
_plotly_js_included = False
_pending = []

def _flush():
    global _pending
    if not _pending: return
    if len(_pending) == 1:
        h, s = _pending[0]
        display(HTML(f'<div style="margin-bottom:2rem;">{h}'
                     f'<p style="font-size:0.73rem;color:#777;margin:3px 0 0 0;">{s}</p></div>'))
    else:
        (h1,s1),(h2,s2) = _pending[0], _pending[1]
        display(HTML(f"""
<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:20px;margin-bottom:2rem;">
  <div>{h1}<p style="font-size:0.73rem;color:#777;margin:3px 0 0 0;">{s1}</p></div>
  <div>{h2}<p style="font-size:0.73rem;color:#777;margin:3px 0 0 0;">{s2}</p></div>
</div>"""))
    _pending = []

def show(fig, source_text, flush=False):
    global _plotly_js_included
    include_js = 'cdn' if not _plotly_js_included else False
    _plotly_js_included = True
    html = fig.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs=include_js,
                       config={'displayModeBar': False})
    _pending.append((html, source_text))
    if flush or len(_pending) == 2:
        _flush()

# ── LLM helper ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TEALBOOK_SYSTEM = """You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing a section
of the Tealbook A — the internal briefing document prepared for FOMC meetings.
Style rules:
- Third person, no first-person pronouns
- Every claim must reference a specific number AND its time period
- Complete every sentence fully — never truncate mid-sentence
- Do not invent numbers; only use what is given
Respond with narrative text only — no headers, no bullet points, no markdown."""

def call_claude(user_prompt, max_tokens=450):
    api_key = os.environ.get('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY', '')
    if not api_key:
        return "_[LLM narrative unavailable — set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to enable.]_"
    payload = json.dumps({
        "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
        "max_tokens": max_tokens,
        "system": TEALBOOK_SYSTEM,
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}]
    }).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages",
        data=payload,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json",
                 "x-api-key": api_key,
                 "anthropic-version": "2023-06-01"}
    )
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
            return json.loads(resp.read())["content"][0]["text"].strip()
    except Exception as e:
        return f"_[LLM call failed: {e}]_"
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Overview

```{python}
#| label: overview-data
#| include: false
import io, requests as _req

gdp_g    = pull_series('A191RL1Q225SBEA', start=DATA_START).dropna()
unrate   = pull_series('UNRATE',          start=DATA_START).dropna()
core_pce = pull_series('PCEPILFE',        start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
pce_ov   = pull_series('PCEPI',           start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
cpi_ov   = pull_series('CPIAUCSL',        start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
payrolls = pull_series('PAYEMS',          start=DATA_START).dropna().diff().dropna()
nrou     = pull_series('NROU',            start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').interpolate()
t10      = pull_series('DGS10',           start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
t2       = pull_series('DGS2',            start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()

gdp_latest    = gdp_g.iloc[-1]
gdp_prev      = gdp_g.iloc[-2]
gdp_date      = quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(gdp_g.index[-1]))
gdp_prev_date = quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(gdp_g.index[-2]))
unrate_latest = unrate.iloc[-1]
unrate_date   = month_label(pd.Timestamp(unrate.index[-1]))
nrou_latest   = nrou.reindex(unrate.index, method='ffill').iloc[-1]
ugap          = unrate_latest - nrou_latest
core_latest   = core_pce.iloc[-1]
core_date     = month_label(pd.Timestamp(core_pce.index[-1]))
pce_latest    = pce_ov.iloc[-1]
payroll_3m    = payrolls.iloc[-3:].mean()
payroll_date  = month_label(pd.Timestamp(payrolls.index[-1]))
t10_latest    = t10.iloc[-1]
t10_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(t10.index[-1]))
spread_10_2   = (t10 - t2).dropna().iloc[-1]

# SPF / FOMC SEP — update each cycle
spf_gdp_next  = 2.1
spf_pce_next  = 2.3
fomc_lr_gdp   = 1.8
fomc_lr_unemp = 4.1
fomc_lr_pce   = 2.0

# ── SPF for comparison table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
SPF_BASE_OV = ('https://www.philadelphiafed.org/-/media/FRBP/Assets/'
               'Surveys-And-Data/survey-of-professional-forecasters/'
               'data-files/files/Median_{}_Growth.xlsx')
SPF_BASE_LV = ('https://www.philadelphiafed.org/-/media/FRBP/Assets/'
               'Surveys-And-Data/survey-of-professional-forecasters/'
               'data-files/files/Median_{}_Level.xlsx')

def _spf_q4(name, row_idx=-1, level=False):
    """
    Pull SPF Q4/Q4 annual forecasts for comparison table.
    level=True for unemployment (level file, column = just name+horizon number).
    Returns dict {year: value}.
    """
    try:
        url_base = SPF_BASE_LV if level else SPF_BASE_OV
        r = _req.get(url_base.format(name), timeout=20)
        r.raise_for_status()
        df = pd.read_excel(io.BytesIO(r.content), engine='openpyxl')
        df.columns = [str(c).strip() for c in df.columns]
        df_c = df.dropna(subset=['YEAR','QUARTER'])
        if len(df_c) < abs(row_idx):
            return {}
        row  = df_c.iloc[row_idx]
        year = int(row['YEAR'])
        qtr  = int(row['QUARTER'])
        # For level series, columns are just NAME+h (e.g. UNEMP2..UNEMP6)
        # For growth series, columns are D+NAME+h (e.g. DRGDP2..DRGDP6)
        prefix = name if level else f'D{name}'
        out = {}
        for h in range(2, 7):
            col = f'{prefix}{h}'
            if col in row.index and pd.notna(row[col]):
                offset = h - 2
                total  = (qtr - 1) + offset
                y_out  = year + total // 4
                q_out  = (total % 4) + 1
                if q_out == 4:   # only Q4 values for comparison table
                    out[y_out] = round(float(row[col]), 1)
        return out
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'[SPF table] {name}: {e}')
        return {}

# Current and prior vintages
spf_gdp_q4_curr   = _spf_q4('RGDP',  row_idx=-1)
spf_gdp_q4_prior  = _spf_q4('RGDP',  row_idx=-2)
spf_unemp_q4_curr  = _spf_q4('UNEMP', row_idx=-1, level=True)
spf_unemp_q4_prior = _spf_q4('UNEMP', row_idx=-2, level=True)
spf_pce_q4_curr   = _spf_q4('PCEPI', row_idx=-1)
spf_pce_q4_prior  = _spf_q4('PCEPI', row_idx=-2)
spf_cpi_q4_curr   = _spf_q4('CPI',   row_idx=-1)
spf_cpi_q4_prior  = _spf_q4('CPI',   row_idx=-2)

# Forecast years from current vintage
_all_yrs = sorted(set(list(spf_gdp_q4_curr.keys()) +
                       list(spf_unemp_q4_curr.keys()) +
                       list(spf_pce_q4_curr.keys())))
_yr1 = _all_yrs[0] if _all_yrs else TODAY.year
_yr2 = _all_yrs[1] if len(_all_yrs) > 1 else _yr1 + 1

def _fq4(d, yr):
    v = d.get(yr)
    return f'{v:.1f}' if v is not None else 'n.a.'
```

```{python}
#| label: overview-comparison-table
from IPython.display import display, HTML

rows_data = [
    ('GDP (Q4/Q4 percent change)', [
        ('Current SPF',  _fq4(spf_gdp_q4_curr,  _yr1), _fq4(spf_gdp_q4_curr,  _yr2)),
        ('Prior SPF',    _fq4(spf_gdp_q4_prior, _yr1), _fq4(spf_gdp_q4_prior, _yr2)),
        ('FOMC LR',      f'{fomc_lr_gdp:.1f}',          f'{fomc_lr_gdp:.1f}'),
    ]),
    ('Unemployment rate (Q4 level)', [
        ('Current SPF',  _fq4(spf_unemp_q4_curr,  _yr1), _fq4(spf_unemp_q4_curr,  _yr2)),
        ('Prior SPF',    _fq4(spf_unemp_q4_prior, _yr1), _fq4(spf_unemp_q4_prior, _yr2)),
        ('FOMC LR',      f'{fomc_lr_unemp:.1f}',           f'{fomc_lr_unemp:.1f}'),
    ]),
    ('CPI inflation (Q4/Q4 percent change)', [
        ('Current SPF',  _fq4(spf_cpi_q4_curr,  _yr1), _fq4(spf_cpi_q4_curr,  _yr2)),
        ('Prior SPF',    _fq4(spf_cpi_q4_prior, _yr1), _fq4(spf_cpi_q4_prior, _yr2)),
    ]),
    ('PCE price inflation (Q4/Q4 percent change)', [
        ('Current SPF',  _fq4(spf_pce_q4_curr,  _yr1), _fq4(spf_pce_q4_curr,  _yr2)),
        ('Prior SPF',    _fq4(spf_pce_q4_prior, _yr1), _fq4(spf_pce_q4_prior, _yr2)),
        ('FOMC target',  f'{fomc_lr_pce:.1f}',           f'{fomc_lr_pce:.1f}'),
    ]),
]

tbl_html = f"""
{tbl_title('Comparison of Current and Prior SPF Forecasts')}
<table style="width:min(65%,560px);border-collapse:collapse;
              font-family:'Source Sans Pro',Arial,sans-serif;font-size:0.88rem;">
  <thead>
    <tr style="background:{C1};color:white;">
      <th style="padding:7px 14px;text-align:left;"></th>
      <th style="padding:7px 12px;text-align:right;font-weight:normal;">{_yr1}</th>
      <th style="padding:7px 12px;text-align:right;font-weight:normal;">{_yr2}</th>
    </tr>
  </thead><tbody>"""

for group_label, sub_rows in rows_data:
    tbl_html += f"""<tr><td colspan="3" style="padding:8px 14px 2px 14px;
        font-weight:bold;font-size:0.88rem;border-top:1px solid #ccc;
        background:#f8f9fb;">{group_label}</td></tr>"""
    for i,(lbl, v1, v2) in enumerate(sub_rows):
        bg  = 'white' if i > 0 else '#eef2fb'
        fw  = 'font-weight:600;' if i == 0 else ''
        tbl_html += f"""<tr style="background:{bg};">
          <td style="padding:4px 14px 4px 28px;{fw}">{lbl}</td>
          <td style="padding:4px 12px;text-align:right;{fw}">{v1}</td>
          <td style="padding:4px 12px;text-align:right;{fw}">{v2}</td>
        </tr>"""

tbl_html += f"""</tbody>
  <tfoot><tr style="background:#f0f0f0;font-size:0.75rem;color:#777;">
    <td colspan="3" style="padding:5px 14px;">
      Source: Philadelphia Fed Survey of Professional Forecasters (SPF).
      FOMC longer-run values from SEP. n.a. = not available in current vintage.
    </td></tr></tfoot></table>"""
display(HTML(tbl_html))
```

```{python}
#| label: overview-narrative
#| eval: false
#| output: asis

prompt = f"""Current economic conditions as of {now_str}:
- Real GDP growth ({gdp_date}, SAAR): {gdp_latest:.1f}%; prior quarter ({gdp_prev_date}): {gdp_prev:.1f}%
- Unemployment rate ({unrate_date}): {unrate_latest:.1f}%; natural rate: {nrou_latest:.1f}%; gap: {ugap:+.1f} pp
- Core PCE inflation YoY ({core_date}): {core_latest:.1f}%; Fed target: {fomc_lr_pce:.1f}%
- Average monthly payroll gains, 3-month avg through {payroll_date}: {payroll_3m:,.0f}K
- 10-year Treasury yield ({t10_date}): {t10_latest:.2f}%; 10s-2s spread: {spread_10_2:+.2f} pp
- SPF: GDP {spf_gdp_next}%, PCE {spf_pce_next}% (next year medians)
- FOMC LR: GDP {fomc_lr_gdp}%, unemployment {fomc_lr_unemp}%, PCE {fomc_lr_pce}%

Write a 2-paragraph overview. Paragraph 1: real activity and labor market.
Paragraph 2: inflation and financial conditions.
Every sentence must include the specific time period it references."""

print(call_claude(prompt))

```

```{python}
#| label: plot-overview-1

from IPython.display import display, HTML

fn = next_fig()
fig1 = go.Figure()
g = gdp_g[CHART_START:]
fig1.add_trace(go.Bar(x=g.index, y=g.values,
    marker_color=[C1 if v>=0 else C2 for v in g], name='GDP growth',
    hovertemplate='%{x|Q%q %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig1.add_hline(y=0, line_width=0.7, line_color='black')
fig1.update_layout(**fed_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Real GDP Growth (%, SAAR)', height=320))
fig1.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')])

fn2 = next_fig()
fig2 = go.Figure()
ur = unrate[CHART_START:]
nr = nrou.reindex(ur.index, method='ffill')
fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ur.index, y=ur.values, name='Unemployment',
    line=line(C1), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=nr.index, y=nr.values, name='Natural rate (CBO)',
    line=line(C2,'dash'), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig2.add_hline(y=fomc_lr_unemp, line_color=C3, line_dash='dot', line_width=1,
    annotation_text=f'LR {fomc_lr_unemp}%', annotation_position='top right')
fig2.update_layout(**fed_layout(f'Figure {fn2} — Unemployment Rate (%)', height=320))
fig2.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')])

h1 = fig1.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs='cdn',  config={'displayModeBar':False})
h2 = fig2.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs=False, config={'displayModeBar':False})
display(HTML(f"""
<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:20px;margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <div>{h1}</div><div>{h2}</div>
</div>"""))
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-overview-2

fn3 = next_fig()
fig3 = go.Figure()
fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=core_pce[CHART_START:].index, y=core_pce[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Core PCE', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=pce_ov[CHART_START:].index, y=pce_ov[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Headline PCE', line=line(C2,'dash'),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig3.add_hline(y=2.0, line_color=C3, line_dash='dot', line_width=1,
    annotation_text='2% target', annotation_position='top right')
fig3.update_layout(**fed_layout(f'Figure {fn3} — PCE Inflation (YoY %)', height=320))
fig3.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, TODAY.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')])

fn4 = next_fig()
fig4 = go.Figure()
fig4.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=unrate[CHART_START:].index, y=unrate[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Unemployment', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
# SPF forecast dots for Q4 values
added_legend = False
for yr, d_curr, d_prior in [(_yr1, spf_unemp_q4_curr, spf_unemp_q4_prior),
                              (_yr2, spf_unemp_q4_curr, spf_unemp_q4_prior)]:
    vc = d_curr.get(yr); vp = d_prior.get(yr)
    if vc:
        fig4.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=[pd.Timestamp(f'{yr}-12-01')], y=[vc], mode='markers',
            name='SPF forecast', marker=dict(color=C2, size=9, symbol='circle'),
            hovertemplate=f'SPF Q4/{yr}: {vc:.1f}%<extra></extra>',
            showlegend=not added_legend))
        added_legend = True
    if vp:
        fig4.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=[pd.Timestamp(f'{yr}-12-01')], y=[vp], mode='markers',
            name='Prior SPF', marker=dict(color=C3, size=9, symbol='circle-open'),
            hovertemplate=f'Prior SPF Q4/{yr}: {vp:.1f}%<extra></extra>',
            showlegend=(yr==_yr1)))
fig4.add_hline(y=fomc_lr_unemp, line_color=C3, line_dash='dot', line_width=1,
    annotation_text=f'LR {fomc_lr_unemp}%', annotation_position='bottom right')
fig4.update_layout(**fed_layout(f'Figure {fn4} — Unemployment: History & SPF Forecast', height=320))
fig4.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, f'{_yr2+1}-01-01'])

h3 = fig3.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs=False, config={'displayModeBar':False})
h4 = fig4.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs=False, config={'displayModeBar':False})
display(HTML(f"""
<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:20px;margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <div>{h3}</div><div>{h4}</div>
</div>"""))
```

---
## Real GDP

```{python}
#| label: gdp-setup
#| include: false

import io
try:
    import requests
except ImportError:
    import subprocess, sys as _sys
    subprocess.check_call([_sys.executable,'-m','pip','install','requests'])
    import requests

SPF_BASE = ('https://www.philadelphiafed.org/-/media/FRBP/Assets/'
            'Surveys-And-Data/survey-of-professional-forecasters/'
            'data-files/files/Median_{}_Growth.xlsx')

def pull_spf(spf_name, row_index=-1):
    try:
        r = requests.get(SPF_BASE.format(spf_name), timeout=20)
        r.raise_for_status()
        df = pd.read_excel(io.BytesIO(r.content), engine='openpyxl')
        df.columns = [str(c).strip() for c in df.columns]
        prefix = f'D{spf_name}'
        col_now = f'{prefix}2'
        df_clean = df.dropna(subset=['YEAR', 'QUARTER'])
        if col_now in df_clean.columns:
            df_clean = df_clean[df_clean[col_now].notna()]
        if df_clean.empty or len(df_clean) < abs(row_index):
            return {}
        row  = df_clean.iloc[row_index]
        year = int(row['YEAR']); qtr = int(row['QUARTER'])
        result = {}
        for h in range(2, 7):
            col = f'{prefix}{h}'
            if col in row.index and pd.notna(row[col]):
                offset = h - 2; total = (qtr-1) + offset
                result[f'Q{(total%4)+1} {year+total//4}'] = round(float(row[col]), 1)
        return result
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'  [WARNING] SPF {spf_name}: {e}')
        return {}

SPF_BASE_LVL = ('https://www.philadelphiafed.org/-/media/FRBP/Assets/'
                'Surveys-And-Data/survey-of-professional-forecasters/'
                'data-files/files/Median_{}_Level.xlsx')

def pull_spf_level(spf_name, row_index=-1):
    """Pull SPF level forecasts (e.g. RCBI, REXPORT, UNEMP).
    Returns dict {quarter_label: level_value}."""
    try:
        r = requests.get(SPF_BASE_LVL.format(spf_name), timeout=20)
        r.raise_for_status()
        df = pd.read_excel(io.BytesIO(r.content), engine='openpyxl')
        df.columns = [str(c).strip() for c in df.columns]
        prefix = spf_name   # level files: RCBI2..RCBI6, REXPORT2..REXPORT6
        col_now = f'{prefix}2'
        df_clean = df.dropna(subset=['YEAR', 'QUARTER'])
        if col_now in df_clean.columns:
            df_clean = df_clean[df_clean[col_now].notna()]
        if df_clean.empty or len(df_clean) < abs(row_index):
            return {}
        row  = df_clean.iloc[row_index]
        year = int(row['YEAR']); qtr = int(row['QUARTER'])
        result = {}
        for h in range(2, 7):
            col = f'{prefix}{h}'
            if col in row.index and pd.notna(row[col]):
                offset = h - 2; total = (qtr-1) + offset
                result[f'Q{(total%4)+1} {year+total//4}'] = round(float(row[col]), 1)
        return result
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'  [WARNING] SPF level {spf_name}: {e}')
        return {}

COMPONENTS = [
    ('Real GDP',                'A191RL1Q225SBEA', 'A191RL1Q225SBEA', 'RGDP'),
    ('PCE',                     'DPCERL1Q225SBEA', 'DPCERY2Q224SBEA', 'RCONSUM'),
    ('Nonres. fixed investment','A008RL1Q225SBEA', 'A008RY2Q224SBEA', 'RNRESIN'),
    ('Residential investment',  'A011RL1Q225SBEA', 'A011RY2Q224SBEA', 'RRESINV'),
    ('Federal government',      'A823RL1Q225SBEA', 'A823RY2Q224SBEA', 'RFEDGOV'),
    ('State & local govt',      'A829RL1Q225SBEA', 'A829RY2Q224SBEA', 'RSLGOV'),
    ('Change in inventories',   None,              'A014RY2Q224SBEA', 'RCBI'),
    ('Net exports',             None,              'A019RY2Q224SBEA', 'REXPORT'),
]

def last2(ticker):
    if ticker is None: return None, None, None, None
    s = pull_series(ticker, start=DATA_START).dropna()
    if len(s) < 2: return None, None, None, None
    return (quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(s.index[-2])), round(float(s.iloc[-2]), 2),
            quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(s.index[-1])), round(float(s.iloc[-1]), 2))

pot   = pull_series('GDPPOT', start=DATA_START).dropna()
pot_g = (((pot / pot.shift(1)) ** 4) - 1).mul(100).dropna()

def pot_for_quarter(ql):
    for dt, val in pot_g.items():
        if quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(dt)) == ql:
            return round(float(val), 1)
    return None

gdp_s       = pull_series('A191RL1Q225SBEA', start=DATA_START).dropna()
gdp_vintage = f"BEA release through {quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(gdp_s.index[-1]))}"

saar_rows=[]; contrib_rows=[]; spf_all={}; spf_prev_all={}

for label, saar_t, contrib_t, spf_name in COMPONENTS:
    qp,vp,qc,vc         = last2(saar_t)
    qp_c,vp_c,qc_c,vc_c = last2(contrib_t)
    spf_d      = pull_spf(spf_name, row_index=-1)
    spf_d_prev = pull_spf(spf_name, row_index=-2)
    spf_all[label]=spf_d; spf_prev_all[label]=spf_d_prev
    bold = (label=='Real GDP')
    saar_rows.append({'label':label,'bold':bold,'q_prev':qp,'v_prev':vp,
                      'q_curr':qc,'v_curr':vc,'spf':spf_d,'spf_prev':spf_d_prev,
                      'no_saar':saar_t is None})
    contrib_rows.append({'label':label,'bold':bold,'q_prev':qp_c,'v_prev':vp_c,
                         'q_curr':qc_c,'v_curr':vc_c})

# RCBI and REXPORT: quarterly contribution forecasts from SPF Level files (chained $B)
spf_rcbi = pull_spf_level('RCBI',    row_index=-1)
spf_rexp = pull_spf_level('REXPORT', row_index=-1)

def _rcbi(q): return spf_rcbi.get(q)
def _rexp(q): return spf_rexp.get(q)

# Realized BEA levels for q_curr (chained $B, SAAR) — for apples-to-apples comparison
_cbi_s    = pull_series('CBI',    start=DATA_START).dropna()
_netexp_s = pull_series('NETEXP', start=DATA_START).dropna()
_cbi_curr    = round(float(_cbi_s.iloc[-1]),    1) if not _cbi_s.empty    else None
_netexp_curr = round(float(_netexp_s.iloc[-1]), 1) if not _netexp_s.empty else None

gdp_row      = saar_rows[0]
q_last       = gdp_row['q_prev']   # last released quarter (context only)
q_curr       = gdp_row['q_curr']   # most recent released quarter
spf_gdp      = spf_all['Real GDP']
spf_gdp_prev = spf_prev_all['Real GDP']
spf_qs       = list(spf_gdp.keys())
gdp_last_v   = gdp_row['v_prev']
gdp_curr_v   = gdp_row['v_curr']

# q_now = current quarter underway (SPF primary target), q_next = following quarter
q_now  = spf_qs[0] if spf_qs else 'Q+1'
q_next = spf_qs[1] if len(spf_qs)>1 else 'Q+2'

def _sv(label, q):  return spf_all.get(label, {}).get(q)
def _spv(label, q): return spf_prev_all.get(label, {}).get(q)
def _cv(label):     # contrib for q_curr (last released)
    r = next((x for x in contrib_rows if x['label']==label), {})
    return r.get('v_curr')
def _av(label):     # saar growth for q_curr
    r = next((x for x in saar_rows if x['label']==label), {})
    return r.get('v_curr')
def _f(v):  return f'{v:.1f}' if v is not None else 'n/a'
def _fp(v): return f'{v:+.1f}' if v is not None else 'n/a'

# _cv_now: for narrative prompts referring to q_now, use SPF as tracked contributions
# (In reality we don't have realized q_now contributions yet — SPF is our best estimate)
def _cv_now(label):
    # Use current SPF forecast as proxy for q_now tracked contribution
    v = _sv(label, q_now)
    return v  # may be None for inventories/net exports

gdp_lvl   = pull_series('GDPC1',  start=DATA_START).dropna()
pot_lvl   = pull_series('GDPPOT', start=DATA_START).dropna()
pot_align = pot_lvl.reindex(gdp_lvl.index, method='ffill')
og_series = ((gdp_lvl - pot_align) / pot_align * 100).dropna()
og_latest = round(float(og_series.iloc[-1]), 2)
og_date   = quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(og_series.index[-1]))

# Private domestic final purchases for q_now (sum of tracked SPF contributions)
pdfp_now = sum(filter(None, [
    _sv('PCE', q_now),
    _sv('Nonres. fixed investment', q_now),
    _sv('Residential investment', q_now),
])) or 0.0

# Driver summary for Beige Book prompt
_drivers = []
for lbl in ['PCE','Nonres. fixed investment','Residential investment','Federal government']:
    v = _sv(lbl, q_now)
    if v is not None:
        _drivers.append(f'{lbl}: {v:+.1f}%')
driver_summary = '; '.join(_drivers) if _drivers else 'SPF forecasts unavailable'
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-beigebook
#| include: false

BEIGE_BOOK_CACHE = Path('../data/beige_book_latest.txt')

def fetch_beige_book():
    try:
        from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta
        import urllib3
        headers = {'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36'}
        now = datetime.today()
        for m in range(12):
            dt = now - relativedelta(months=m)
            yyyymm = dt.strftime('%Y%m')
            url = f"https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/beigebook{yyyymm}-summary.htm"
            for verify in [True, False]:
                try:
                    r = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=15, verify=verify)
                    if r.status_code == 404: break
                    if r.status_code != 200: continue
                    text  = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>',' ',r.text)
                    text  = re.sub(r'\s+',' ',text).strip()
                    start = re.search(r'Economic activity',text,re.IGNORECASE)
                    end   = re.search(r'Federal Reserve Bank of Boston',text,re.IGNORECASE)
                    if start and end and end.start()>start.start():
                        summary = text[start.start():end.start()].strip()
                    elif start:
                        summary = text[start.start():start.start()+2000].strip()
                    else: break
                    if len(summary)<100: break
                    if len(summary)>2000: summary=summary[:2000]+'...'
                    dm = re.search(r'(January|February|March|April|May|June|July|August|'
                                   r'September|October|November|December)\s+\d{1,2},?\s+\d{4}',text)
                    bb_date = dm.group(0) if dm else dt.strftime('%B %Y')
                    BEIGE_BOOK_CACHE.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                    BEIGE_BOOK_CACHE.write_text(f'{bb_date}\n{summary}',encoding='utf-8')
                    print(f'[Beige Book] Fetched: {bb_date}')
                    return summary, bb_date
                except requests.exceptions.SSLError:
                    if verify: urllib3.disable_warnings(); continue
                    break
                except Exception as e:
                    print(f'[BB] {yyyymm}: {e}'); break
        if BEIGE_BOOK_CACHE.exists():
            lines = BEIGE_BOOK_CACHE.read_text(encoding='utf-8').split('\n',1)
            if len(lines)==2 and len(lines[1])>100:
                print(f'[Beige Book] From cache: {lines[0]}')
                return lines[1].strip(), lines[0].strip()
        print('[WARNING] Beige Book unavailable'); return None,None
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'[WARNING] Beige Book failed: {e}'); return None,None

beige_text, beige_date = fetch_beige_book()
```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-1
#| eval: false
#| output: asis

pdfp_now_str = f'{pdfp_now:+.1f}' if pdfp_now else 'n/a'

prompt_1 = f"""
You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing the GDP & Output section of Tealbook A.
ANCHORING RULE:
- The main object is GDP growth in {q_now}, which has not yet been released.
- Use {q_curr} only as context — do not write a retrospective paragraph about it.
Style:
- Declarative, precise, third person.
- Write one coherent paragraph with complete sentences.
- Use numerical values where most informative.
- Use cautious causal language (e.g., "consistent with", "suggesting", "likely reflecting").
DATA:
SPF median GDP forecast:
- {q_now}: {_f(spf_gdp.get(q_now))}%
- {q_next}: {_f(spf_gdp.get(q_next))}%
Realized GDP (context only):
- {q_curr}: {_f(gdp_curr_v)}%
Tracked SPF contributions for {q_now} (pp):
- PCE: {_f(_sv('PCE', q_now))}
- Nonresidential investment: {_f(_sv('Nonres. fixed investment', q_now))}
- Residential investment: {_f(_sv('Residential investment', q_now))}
- Government (federal SPF): {_f(_sv('Federal government', q_now))}
Private domestic final purchases ({q_now}): {pdfp_now_str} pp
TASK:
Write exactly 1 paragraph (4 sentences):
1. State the SPF-based estimate for GDP growth in {q_now} and compare it to realized GDP in {q_curr}.
2. Indicate whether underlying momentum in {q_now} appears stronger or weaker than headline GDP using private domestic final purchases.
3. Identify the main sources of support or drag in the {q_now} estimate.
4. State the SPF forecast for {q_next} and whether it implies firming or moderation relative to {q_now}.
"""
print(call_claude(prompt_1, max_tokens=320))

```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-2
#| eval: false
#| output: asis

prompt_2 = f"""
You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing the GDP & Output section of Tealbook A.
ANCHORING RULE:
- Describe the expected composition of GDP growth in {q_now}, not the realized breakdown of {q_curr}.
Style:
- Declarative, precise, third person.
- Write 2 short paragraphs (no bullets).
- Use numerical values where most informative.
TRACKED CONTRIBUTIONS TO {q_now} GDP (pp, from SPF):
- PCE: {_f(_sv('PCE', q_now))}
- Nonresidential investment: {_f(_sv('Nonres. fixed investment', q_now))}
- Residential investment: {_f(_sv('Residential investment', q_now))}
- Federal government: {_f(_sv('Federal government', q_now))}
- State & local government: {_f(_sv('State & local govt', q_now))}
- Inventories ({q_curr} realized): {_fp(_cv('Change in inventories'))}
- Net exports ({q_curr} realized): {_fp(_cv('Net exports'))}
TASK:
Paragraph 1:
Describe the main drivers of GDP growth in {q_now}, focusing on consumption and investment.
Paragraph 2:
Explain the role of inventories and net exports in {q_now}, and whether they materially distort headline GDP relative to underlying demand.
"""
print(call_claude(prompt_2, max_tokens=320))

```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-3
#| eval: false
#| output: asis

prompt_3 = f"""
You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing Tealbook A.
ANCHORING RULE:
- Focus on expected developments in {q_now} and {q_next}.
- Use {q_curr} only as context.
Style:
- Declarative, precise, third person.
- Write 2 short paragraphs.
- Use forward-looking language ("is projected to", "appears on track to").
DATA:
PCE:
- SPF: {q_now}: {_f(_sv('PCE', q_now))}%, {q_next}: {_f(_sv('PCE', q_next))}%
- Prior SPF: {q_now}: {_f(_spv('PCE', q_now))}%
- Latest realized ({q_curr}): {_f(_av('PCE'))}%
Residential investment:
- SPF: {q_now}: {_f(_sv('Residential investment', q_now))}%, {q_next}: {_f(_sv('Residential investment', q_next))}%
- Prior SPF: {q_now}: {_f(_spv('Residential investment', q_now))}%
- Latest realized ({q_curr}): {_f(_av('Residential investment'))}%
TASK:
Paragraph 1:
Describe the outlook for consumer spending in {q_now} and {q_next}, using {q_curr} only to characterize recent momentum.
Note whether the SPF was revised up or down relative to the prior vintage.
Paragraph 2:
Describe the outlook for residential investment in {q_now} and {q_next}, noting whether activity appears to be stabilizing or continuing to contract.
"""
print(call_claude(prompt_3, max_tokens=340))

```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-4
#| eval: false
#| output: asis

prompt_4 = f"""
You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing Tealbook A.
ANCHORING RULE:
- Focus on expected contributions in {q_now}.
- Do not write a backward-looking decomposition of {q_curr}.
Style:
- Declarative, precise, third person.
- Write 3 short paragraphs (no bullets).
DATA:
Nonresidential investment:
- SPF: {q_now}: {_f(_sv('Nonres. fixed investment', q_now))}%, {q_next}: {_f(_sv('Nonres. fixed investment', q_next))}%
- Prior SPF {q_now}: {_f(_spv('Nonres. fixed investment', q_now))}%
Government:
- Federal contribution ({q_now} SPF): {_f(_sv('Federal government', q_now))}%
- State & local ({q_now} SPF): {_f(_sv('State & local govt', q_now))}%
- Federal realized ({q_curr}): {_fp(_cv('Federal government'))} pp
External — inventories (chained $B SAAR, for direct comparison):
- Realized BEA level {q_curr} (CBI): {_f(_cbi_curr)}B
- SPF forecast {q_now} (RCBI): {_f(_rcbi(q_now))}B
- SPF forecast {q_next} (RCBI): {_f(_rcbi(q_next))}B
- Realized pp contribution {q_curr} (context only): {_fp(_cv('Change in inventories'))} pp
External — net exports (chained $B SAAR, for direct comparison):
- Realized BEA level {q_curr} (NETEXP): {_f(_netexp_curr)}B
- SPF forecast {q_now} (REXPORT): {_f(_rexp(q_now))}B
- SPF forecast {q_next} (REXPORT): {_f(_rexp(q_next))}B
- Realized pp contribution {q_curr} (context only): {_fp(_cv('Net exports'))} pp
TASK:
Paragraph 1:
Describe the outlook for business fixed investment in {q_now} and {q_next}, noting whether it is expected to strengthen or weaken relative to {q_curr}.
Paragraph 2:
Describe the contribution of government spending in {q_now} and whether it represents a positive or negative impulse to growth.
Paragraph 3:
All values below are in chained dollars (SAAR), enabling direct comparison.
- For inventories: compare the realized BEA level in {q_curr} to the SPF forecast for {q_now}.
  If {q_now} is higher than {q_curr}, inventories are expected to accumulate (positive GDP contribution);
  if lower, a drawdown is expected (negative contribution). State the direction and approximate magnitude.
- For net exports: compare the realized BEA level in {q_curr} to the SPF forecast for {q_now}.
  A more negative (or less positive) value in {q_now} vs {q_curr} implies a larger drag on GDP.
  State the direction and approximate magnitude.
- Conclude whether these two components together are likely to add to or subtract from
  {q_now} headline GDP relative to underlying final demand.
Do not mix percentage point contributions with chained dollar levels in the same sentence.
"""
print(call_claude(prompt_4, max_tokens=450))

```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-growth-table
#| output: asis
from IPython.display import display, HTML

def signed(v, bold=False):
    if v is None or (isinstance(v,float) and np.isnan(v)):
        return '<span style="color:#ccc">—</span>'
    color = C1 if v>0 else (C2 if v<0 else '#333')
    s = f'{v:+.1f}'
    return (f'<strong style="color:{color}">{s}</strong>'
            if bold else f'<span style="color:{color}">{s}</span>')

def plain(v, bold=False):
    if v is None or (isinstance(v,float) and np.isnan(v)):
        return '<span style="color:#ccc">—</span>'
    s = f'{v:.1f}'
    return f'<strong>{s}</strong>' if bold else s

all_q_labels = [q_last, q_curr] + spf_qs
hdrs    = ['Measure'] + all_q_labels
act_set = {q_last, q_curr}

header_cells = ''.join(
    f'<th style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:{"left" if i==0 else "center"};'
    f'white-space:nowrap;'
    f'{"background:#003087;" if ql in act_set or i==0 else "background:#1a5276;"}'
    f'color:white;font-size:0.82rem;">'
    f'{"Measure" if i==0 else (ql if ql in act_set else ql+"f")}</th>'
    for i,ql in enumerate(hdrs)
)

rows_html = ''
for r in saar_rows:
    if r['no_saar']: continue
    b  = r['bold']
    bg = '#eef2fb' if b else 'white'
    lbl = f'<strong>{r["label"]}</strong>' if b else f'&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;{r["label"]}'
    cells = (f'<td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:left;'
             f'border-bottom:none;font-size:0.87rem;">{lbl}</td>')
    for ql in all_q_labels:
        if ql==q_last:   v=r['v_prev']
        elif ql==q_curr: v=r['v_curr']
        else:            v=r['spf'].get(ql)
        is_fcast = ql not in act_set
        fmt_v    = plain(v,b)
        c_style  = 'color:#1a5276;' if is_fcast and v is not None else ''
        cells += (f'<td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:center;'
                  f'border-bottom:none;font-size:0.87rem;{c_style}">{fmt_v}</td>')
    rows_html += f'<tr style="background:{bg};">{cells}</tr>'
    if not b:
        sub = (f'<td style="padding:1px 10px 5px 20px;text-align:left;'
               f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.78rem;color:#888;'
               f'font-style:italic;">Prior forecast</td>')
        for ql in all_q_labels:
            if ql not in act_set:
                pv = r['spf_prev'].get(ql)
                sub += (f'<td style="padding:1px 10px 5px;text-align:center;'
                        f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.78rem;'
                        f'color:#888;font-style:italic;">{plain(pv)}</td>')
            else:
                sub += (f'<td style="padding:1px 10px 5px;text-align:center;'
                        f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.78rem;color:#ccc;">—</td>')
        rows_html += f'<tr style="background:{bg};">{sub}</tr>'

rows_html += f"""<tr style="background:#f0f4ff;">
  <td colspan="{len(hdrs)}" style="padding:6px 10px;font-size:0.78rem;
     font-weight:bold;color:#555;border-top:2px solid #ccc;border-bottom:1px solid #eee;">
     Contributions (pp) — inventories &amp; net exports
  </td></tr>"""
for r in saar_rows:
    if not r['no_saar']: continue
    cr  = next((x for x in contrib_rows if x['label']==r['label']),{})
    lbl = f'&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;{r["label"]}'
    cells = (f'<td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:left;'
             f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.87rem;">{lbl}</td>')
    for ql in all_q_labels:
        if ql==q_last:   v=cr.get('v_prev')
        elif ql==q_curr: v=cr.get('v_curr')
        else:            v=None
        fmt_v = signed(v) if v is not None else '<span style="color:#ccc">—</span>'
        cells += (f'<td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:center;'
                  f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.87rem;">{fmt_v}</td>')
    rows_html += f'<tr style="background:white;">{cells}</tr>'

pot_cells = ('<td style="padding:6px 10px;text-align:left;border-bottom:1px solid #eee;'
             'font-size:0.87rem;color:#555;font-style:italic;">'
             '&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Potential GDP growth (CBO)</td>')
for ql in all_q_labels:
    pv = pot_for_quarter(ql)
    pot_cells += (f'<td style="padding:6px 10px;text-align:center;'
                  f'border-bottom:1px solid #eee;font-size:0.87rem;'
                  f'color:#555;font-style:italic;">{plain(pv)}</td>')
rows_html += f'<tr style="background:#f8f9ff;">{pot_cells}</tr>'

display(HTML(f"""
{tbl_title('Real GDP Components — Percent Change at Annual Rate (SAAR)')}
<table style="width:min(95%,960px);border-collapse:collapse;
              font-family:'Source Sans Pro',Arial,sans-serif;">
  <thead><tr>{header_cells}</tr></thead>
  <tbody>{rows_html}</tbody>
</table>
<p style="font-size:0.75rem;color:#888;margin:4px 0 1.2rem 0;">
  Source: Actuals from BEA ({gdp_vintage}); Forecasts from Philadelphia Fed SPF median (columns marked <em>f</em>).<br>
  <em>Prior forecast</em> sub-rows show previous quarter's SPF vintage.
  Inventories and net exports: realized contributions (pp) from BEA; SPF quarterly forecasts from Philadelphia Fed RCBI/REXPORT Level files.
  Italicized row: CBO potential GDP growth rate (GDPPOT).
</p>"""))

```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-5
#| eval: false
#| output: asis

prompt_5 = f"""
You are a Federal Reserve staff economist writing Tealbook A.
ANCHORING RULE:
- Focus on the medium-term path for GDP growth.
- Use {q_curr} only as baseline context.
Style: declarative, precise, third person. Complete all sentences.

Output gap ({og_date}): {og_latest:+.2f}% of potential GDP (CBO)
Potential GDP growth: {_f(pot_for_quarter(q_curr))}%
FOMC longer-run real GDP: {fomc_lr_gdp}%
Latest actual GDP ({q_curr}): {_f(gdp_curr_v)}%
SPF forecasts: {' | '.join(f'{q}: {_f(spf_gdp.get(q))}%' for q in spf_qs)}

TASK — Write exactly 1 paragraph (3 complete sentences):
1. State the output gap level as of {og_date}. If positive, note the economy is operating above potential
   in LEVEL terms, while also noting whether the current growth RATE is above or below potential growth,
   which determines whether the gap is widening or narrowing.
2. Compare the SPF growth trajectory to the CBO potential growth rate, stating explicitly whether
   the gap is projected to widen or narrow over the forecast horizon.
3. Name one specific upside risk and one specific downside risk to this trajectory,
   each tied to a named GDP component.
"""
print(call_claude(prompt_5, max_tokens=260))

```

```{python}
#| label: gdp-narrative-6-bb
#| eval: false
#| output: asis

if not beige_text:
    print('*[Beige Book unavailable — contextual note skipped.]*')
else:
    prompt_bb = f"""
You are adding one optional qualitative sentence to a Tealbook A paragraph.
RULES:
- Use the Beige Book only for {q_now}.
- Do not use it to describe {q_curr}.
- Do not infer numerical magnitudes.
- If no clear alignment exists, return exactly: OMIT
GDP outlook ({q_now}): {_f(spf_gdp.get(q_now))}%
Main drivers: {driver_summary}
Beige Book ({beige_date}):
{beige_text[:1200]}
TASK:
Write exactly 2-3 sentences. Do NOT simply confirm the GDP headline.
Instead:
1. Extract the single most concrete, specific qualitative driver mentioned in the Beige Book
   (e.g. a specific sector, demand source, or cost pressure — not "mixed activity").
2. Link it to a specific GDP component in {q_now} using cautious language
   ("consistent with", "suggesting", "may weigh on", "appears to support").
3. If a second distinct driver is prominent and affects a different component, add one more sentence.
Omit generic characterizations like "activity grew modestly in most districts."
If no concrete driver is identifiable, return exactly: OMIT
"""
    result = call_claude(prompt_bb, max_tokens=120).strip()
    if result.upper() != 'OMIT':
        print(f'\n*{result}*\n')

```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-1

gdp_yoy  = pull_series('GDPC1',           start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(4).mul(100).dropna()
gdi_raw  = pull_series('A261RX1Q020SBEA', start=DATA_START).dropna()
gdi_yoy  = gdi_raw.pct_change(4).mul(100).dropna()
gdi_date = quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(gdi_yoy.index[-1]))

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=gdp_yoy[CHART_START_GDP:].index,
    y=gdp_yoy[CHART_START_GDP:].values, mode='lines',
    name='Real GDP', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%Y Q%q}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=gdi_yoy[CHART_START_GDP:].index,
    y=gdi_yoy[CHART_START_GDP:].values, mode='lines',
    name=f'Real GDI (thru {gdi_date})', line=line(C2,'dash'),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%Y Q%q}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='black', line_width=0.8)
fig.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Real GDP & GDI', subtitle='4-quarter % change'))
fig.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
show(fig, 'Source: BEA (GDPC1, A261RX1Q020SBEA) via FRED')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-2

mfg_raw = pull_series('IPXXX001S', start=DATA_START).dropna()
mfg_3m  = (((mfg_raw/mfg_raw.shift(3))**4)-1).mul(100).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=mfg_3m.index, y=mfg_3m.values, mode='lines',
    name='Mfg IP ex. motor vehicles', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='black', line_width=0.8)
fig.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Manufacturing IP ex. Motor Vehicles and Parts',
    subtitle='3-month percent change, annual rate'), showlegend=False)
fig.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
show(fig, 'Source: FRB (IPXXX001S) via FRED', flush=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-3

# ALTSALES: light vehicle sales, millions of units SAAR
# MVAAUTLTTS: total motor vehicle assemblies, millions of units SAAR — same scale, no dual axis
lv       = pull_series('ALTSALES',   start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]
auto_asm = pull_series('MVAAUTLTTS', start=DATA_START).dropna()

# Fallback: DAUPNSA (domestic auto production, thousands of units monthly) if MVAAUTLTTS fails
if auto_asm.empty:
    _raw = pull_series('DAUPNSA', start=DATA_START).dropna()
    # Convert from thousands to millions, resample to monthly average
    auto_asm = (_raw / 1000).resample('MS').mean() if not _raw.empty else _raw

auto_asm = auto_asm[CHART_START_GDP:] if not auto_asm.empty else auto_asm

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=lv.index, y=lv.values, mode='lines',
    name='Sales', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}M<extra></extra>'))
if not auto_asm.empty:
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=auto_asm.index, y=auto_asm.values, mode='lines',
        name='Assemblies', line=line(C2),
        hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}M<extra></extra>'))
layout = base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Sales and Production of Light Motor Vehicles',
                     subtitle='Millions of units, annual rate (SAAR)')
layout['yaxis']['autorange'] = True
fig.update_layout(**layout)
src = 'Source: BEA (ALTSALES), FRB (MVAAUTLTTS) via FRED'
show(fig, src)
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-4

pce_m  = pull_series('PCEC96', start=DATA_START).dropna()
pce_6m = (((pce_m/pce_m.shift(6))**2)-1).mul(100).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=pce_6m.index, y=pce_6m.values, mode='lines',
    name='Real PCE', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='black', line_width=0.8)
fig.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Real PCE Growth',
    subtitle='6-month percent change, annual rate'))
fig.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
show(fig, 'Source: BEA (PCEC96) via FRED', flush=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-5

sf_starts  = pull_series('HOUST1F', start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]
sf_permits = pull_series('PERMIT1', start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=sf_starts.index, y=sf_starts.values, mode='lines',
    name='Starts', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.0f}K<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=sf_permits.index, y=sf_permits.values, mode='lines',
    name='Permits', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.0f}K<extra></extra>'))
fig.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Single-Family Housing Starts and Permits',
    subtitle='Thousands of units, annual rate'))
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=',')
show(fig, 'Note: Tealbook uses adjusted permits.<br>Source: Census (HOUST1F, PERMIT1) via FRED')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-6

exist  = pull_series('EXHOSLUSM495S', start='2003-01-01').dropna() / 1000
new_sf = pull_series('HSN1F',         start='2003-01-01').dropna() / 1000

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=exist.index, y=exist.values, mode='lines',
    name='Existing (left)', yaxis='y1', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}M<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=new_sf.index, y=new_sf.values, mode='lines',
    name='New SF (right)', yaxis='y2', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}M<extra></extra>'))
layout = base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Home Sales',
    subtitle='Millions of units (annual rate)', start='2025-01-01', r_margin=55)
layout['yaxis']  = dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor='#eeeeee', zeroline=False,
                         tickfont=dict(size=9), autorange=True, tickformat='.2f')
layout['yaxis2'] = dict(overlaying='y', side='right', showgrid=False,
                         zeroline=False, tickfont=dict(size=9), autorange=True, tickformat='.2f')
layout['legend'] = dict(orientation='h', yanchor='top', y=-0.18,
                         xanchor='center', x=0.5, font=dict(size=9), bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
layout['margin'] = dict(l=55, r=55, t=65, b=50)
fig.update_layout(**layout)
fig.update_xaxes(type='date', range=['2025-01-01', pd.Timestamp.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')],
                 dtick='M2', tickformat='%b %Y')
show(fig, 'Source: NAR (EXHOSLUSM495S) and Census (HSN1F) via FRED.', flush=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-7

cap_o = (pull_series('NEWORDER', start=DATA_START).dropna().rolling(3).mean()/1000)[CHART_START_GDP:]
cap_s = (pull_series('ANXAVS',   start=DATA_START).dropna().rolling(3).mean()/1000)[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=cap_o.index, y=cap_o.values, mode='lines',
    name='Orders', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: $%{y:.1f}B<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=cap_s.index, y=cap_s.values, mode='lines',
    name='Shipments', line=line(C3),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: $%{y:.1f}B<extra></extra>'))
fig.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Nondefense Cap. Goods ex. Aircraft',
    subtitle='Ratio scale, billions of dollars (3-mo MA)'))
fig.update_yaxes(type='log', tickformat='.0f')
show(fig, 'Note: Data are 3-month moving averages.<br>Source: Census (NEWORDER, ANXAVS) via FRED.')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-8

nrc = (pull_series('TLNRESCONS', start=DATA_START).dropna()/1000)[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=nrc.index, y=nrc.values, mode='lines',
    name='Nonres. construction', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: $%{y:.1f}B<extra></extra>'))
layout = base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Nonresidential Construction Put in Place',
    subtitle='Billions of dollars')
layout['yaxis']['autorange'] = True
fig.update_layout(**layout)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=',')
show(fig, 'Note: Nominal; Tealbook uses real BEA with staff deflator.<br>Source: Census (TLNRESCONS) via FRED.',
     flush=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-9

inv_r = pull_series('ISRATIO', start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=inv_r.index, y=inv_r.values, mode='lines',
    name='Inv/Sales ratio', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f} mo<extra></extra>'))
layout = base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Inventory Ratios', subtitle='Months')
layout['yaxis']['autorange'] = True
fig.update_layout(**layout)
show(fig, 'Note: Staff also calculates a flow-of-goods system.<br>Source: Census (ISRATIO) via FRED.')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-gdp-10

exports = pull_series('EXPGSC1',         start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]
imports = pull_series('A187RX1Q020SBEA', start=DATA_START).dropna()[CHART_START_GDP:]

fn = next_fig()
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=exports.index, y=exports.values, mode='lines',
    name='Exports', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%Y Q%q}: $%{y:,.0f}B<extra></extra>'))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=imports.index, y=imports.values, mode='lines',
    name='Non-oil imports', line=line(C2,'dash'),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%Y Q%q}: $%{y:,.0f}B<extra></extra>'))
layout = base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Exports and Non-oil Imports',
    subtitle='Billions of chained 2017$, SAAR')
layout['yaxis']['autorange'] = True
fig.update_layout(**layout)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=',')
show(fig, 'Source: BEA (EXPGSC1, A187RX1Q020SBEA) via FRED.', flush=True)
```

---

## Labor Market

```{python}
#| label: labor-data
#| include: false

payrolls   = pull_series('PAYEMS',        start=DATA_START).dropna().diff().dropna()
unrate     = pull_series('UNRATE',        start=DATA_START).dropna()
nrou       = pull_series('NROU',          start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').interpolate()
lfpr       = pull_series('CIVPART',       start=DATA_START).dropna()
epop25     = pull_series('LNS12300060',   start=DATA_START).dropna()
ahe        = pull_series('CES0500000003', start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
jolts_o    = pull_series('JTSJOR',        start=DATA_START).dropna().rolling(3).mean()
jolts_q    = pull_series('JTSQUR',        start=DATA_START).dropna().rolling(3).mean()
jolts_h    = pull_series('JTS1000HIR',    start=DATA_START).dropna().rolling(3).mean()
claims     = pull_series('IC4WSA',        start=DATA_START).dropna()

unrate_latest  = unrate.iloc[-1]
unrate_date    = month_label(pd.Timestamp(unrate.index[-1]))
nrou_latest    = nrou.reindex(unrate.index, method='ffill').iloc[-1]
ugap           = unrate_latest - nrou_latest
payroll_3m     = payrolls.iloc[-3:].mean()
payroll_12m    = payrolls.iloc[-12:].mean()
payroll_date   = month_label(pd.Timestamp(payrolls.index[-1]))
lfpr_latest    = lfpr.iloc[-1]
lfpr_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(lfpr.index[-1]))
ahe_latest     = ahe.iloc[-1]
ahe_date       = month_label(pd.Timestamp(ahe.index[-1]))
claims_latest  = claims.iloc[-1]
claims_date    = month_label(pd.Timestamp(claims.index[-1]))
```

```{python}
#| label: labor-narrative
#| eval: false
#| output: asis

prompt = f"""Labor market data:
- Unemployment rate ({unrate_date}): {unrate_latest:.1f}%; natural rate: {nrou_latest:.1f}%; gap: {ugap:+.1f} pp
- Nonfarm payrolls — 3-month avg through {payroll_date}: {payroll_3m:,.0f}K; 12-month avg: {payroll_12m:,.0f}K
- Labor force participation rate ({lfpr_date}): {lfpr_latest:.1f}%
- Average hourly earnings YoY ({ahe_date}): {ahe_latest:.1f}%
- Initial UI claims 4-week MA ({claims_date}): {claims_latest:,.0f}
- FOMC longer-run unemployment: {fomc_lr_unemp}%

Write 2 paragraphs. Paragraph 1: payroll growth, unemployment gap, labor market tightness.
Paragraph 2: wage growth, participation, and the outlook vs. FOMC longer-run.
Every sentence must name the specific month or period it references."""

print(call_claude(prompt))

```

```{python}
#| label: plot-labor-1

# Row 1: Payrolls + Unemployment
fn = next_fig()
figA = go.Figure()
p = payrolls[CHART_START:]
figA.add_trace(go.Bar(x=p.index, y=p.values,
    marker_color=[C1 if v>=0 else C2 for v in p], name='Payrolls',
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:,.0f}K<extra></extra>'))
figA.add_hline(y=0, line_width=0.8, line_color='black')
figA.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Nonfarm Payrolls',
    subtitle='Monthly change, thousands, SA'))
figA.update_yaxes(tickformat=',')

fn2 = next_fig()
figB = go.Figure()
ur = unrate[CHART_START:]
nr = nrou.reindex(ur.index, method='ffill')
figB.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ur.index, y=ur.values, name='U-3',
    line=line(C1), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
figB.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=nr.index, y=nr.values, name='Natural rate (CBO)',
    line=line(C2), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
figB.add_hline(y=fomc_lr_unemp, line_color=C3, line_dash='dash', line_width=1.2,
    annotation_text=f'LR {fomc_lr_unemp}%', annotation_position='top right')
figB.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn2} — Unemployment Rate',
    subtitle='Percent, SA'))
figB.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
show(figA, 'Source: BLS (PAYEMS) via FRED')
show(figB, 'Source: BLS (UNRATE), CBO (NROU) via FRED')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-labor-2

# Row 2: AHE + JOLTS
fn3 = next_fig()
figC = go.Figure()
a = ahe[CHART_START:]
figC.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=a.index, y=a.values, name='AHE YoY',
    line=line(C1), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
figC.add_hline(y=0, line_width=0.8, line_color='black')
figC.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn3} — Avg Hourly Earnings',
    subtitle='Year-over-year percent change'))
figC.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')

fn4 = next_fig()
figD = go.Figure()
for s, name, color, dash in [
    (jolts_o, 'Openings', C1, 'solid'),
    (jolts_q, 'Quits',    C2, 'solid'),
    (jolts_h, 'Hires',    C3, None),
]:
    s2 = s[CHART_START:].dropna()
    figD.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=s2.index, y=s2.values, name=name,
        line=line(color, dash), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
figD.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn4} — JOLTS Rates',
    subtitle='Percent, 3-month moving average'))
figD.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
show(figC, 'Source: BLS (CES0500000003) via FRED')
show(figD, 'Source: BLS (JTSJOR, JTSQUR, JTS1000HIR) via FRED')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-labor-3

# Row 3: LFPR/E-Pop + Claims
fn5 = next_fig()
figE = go.Figure()
lf = lfpr[CHART_START:]; ep = epop25[CHART_START:]
figE.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=lf.index, y=lf.values, name='LFPR (total)',
    line=line(C1), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
figE.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ep.index, y=ep.values, name='E-Pop 25-54',
    line=line(C2), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
figE.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn5} — Labor Force Participation & E-Pop',
    subtitle='Percent'))
figE.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')

fn6 = next_fig()
figF = go.Figure()
c = claims[CHART_START:]
figF.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=c.index, y=c.values, name='Initial claims',
    line=line(C5), hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:,.0f}<extra></extra>'))
figF.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn6} — Initial UI Claims',
    subtitle='Thousands, 4-week moving average'))
figF.update_yaxes(tickformat=',')
show(figE, 'Source: BLS (CIVPART, LNS12300060) via FRED')
show(figF, 'Source: DOL (IC4WSA) via FRED')
```

---

## Inflation

```{python}
#| label: inflation-data
#| include: false

from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

cpi      = pull_series('CPIAUCSL',             start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
pce      = pull_series('PCEPI',                start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
core_pce = pull_series('PCEPILFE',             start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
trim_pce = pull_series('PCETRIM12M159SFRBDAL', start=DATA_START).dropna()
mkt_pce  = pull_series('DPCXRG3M086SBEA',      start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
eci      = pull_series('ECIWAG',               start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(4).mul(100).dropna()
ahe      = pull_series('CES0500000003',         start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
compnfb  = pull_series('COMPNFB',              start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(4).mul(100).dropna()
brent    = pull_series('DCOILBRENTEU',          start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean().dropna()
crb_prx  = pull_series('PALLFNFINDEXM',               start=DATA_START).dropna()
pce_eng  = pull_series('DNRGRG3M086SBEA', start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
imp_core = pull_series('IREXFDFLS',       start=DATA_START).dropna().pct_change(12).mul(100).dropna()
tips_fwd = pull_series('T5YIFRM',              start=DATA_START).dropna()
clev10   = pull_series('EXPINF10YR',           start=DATA_START).dropna()
mich1yr  = pull_series('MICH',                 start=DATA_START).dropna()
recession= pull_series('USREC',               start=DATA_START)

cpi_latest      = cpi.iloc[-1];       cpi_date_inf    = month_label(pd.Timestamp(cpi.index[-1]))
pce_latest      = pce.iloc[-1];       pce_date_inf    = month_label(pd.Timestamp(pce.index[-1]))
core_latest     = core_pce.iloc[-1];  core_date_inf   = month_label(pd.Timestamp(core_pce.index[-1]))
trim_latest     = trim_pce.iloc[-1];  trim_date       = month_label(pd.Timestamp(trim_pce.index[-1]))
mkt_latest      = mkt_pce.iloc[-1];   mkt_date        = month_label(pd.Timestamp(mkt_pce.index[-1]))
eci_latest      = eci.iloc[-1];       eci_date        = quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(eci.index[-1]))
ahe_latest      = ahe.iloc[-1];       ahe_date        = month_label(pd.Timestamp(ahe.index[-1]))
compnfb_latest  = compnfb.iloc[-1];   compnfb_date    = quarter_label(pd.Timestamp(compnfb.index[-1]))
brent_latest    = brent.iloc[-1];     brent_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(brent.index[-1]))
pce_eng_latest  = pce_eng.iloc[-1];  pce_eng_date  = month_label(pd.Timestamp(pce_eng.index[-1]))
imp_core_latest = imp_core.iloc[-1]; imp_core_date = month_label(pd.Timestamp(imp_core.index[-1]))
tips_latest     = tips_fwd.iloc[-1];  tips_date       = month_label(pd.Timestamp(tips_fwd.index[-1]))
clev10_latest   = clev10.iloc[-1];    clev10_date     = month_label(pd.Timestamp(clev10.index[-1]))
mich1yr_latest  = mich1yr.iloc[-1];   mich1yr_date    = month_label(pd.Timestamp(mich1yr.index[-1]))

# ── Shared helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def recession_shapes(rec_series, chart_start):
    rec = rec_series[chart_start:].copy()
    shapes, in_rec, start_date = [], False, None
    for date, val in rec.items():
        if val == 1 and not in_rec:
            in_rec, start_date = True, date
        elif val == 0 and in_rec:
            in_rec = False
            shapes.append(dict(type='rect', xref='x', yref='paper',
                               x0=start_date, x1=date, y0=0, y1=1,
                               fillcolor='#C8C8C8', opacity=0.35,
                               line_width=0, layer='below'))
    if in_rec:
        shapes.append(dict(type='rect', xref='x', yref='paper',
                           x0=start_date, x1=rec.index[-1], y0=0, y1=1,
                           fillcolor='#C8C8C8', opacity=0.35,
                           line_width=0, layer='below'))
    return shapes

def x_end(series, extra_months=5):
    return (pd.Timestamp(series.index[-1]) + pd.DateOffset(months=extra_months)).strftime('%Y-%m-%d')

# ── Date-derived labels for projection table ──────────────────────────────────
_today   = TODAY
_yr      = _today.year
_prev_yr = _yr - 1
_next_yr = _yr + 1
_far_yr  = _yr + 2
_h1      = f"H1 {_yr}"
_h2      = f"H2 {_yr}"
_m = [(_today - relativedelta(months=i)).strftime("%b %Y") for i in range(2, -1, -1)]
_f = [(_today + relativedelta(months=i)).strftime("%b %Y") for i in range(0, 3)]
```

```{python}
#| label: inflation-narrative
#| output: asis

from IPython.display import display, Markdown
import json
import urllib.request
import os
import re


def call_claude_search(user_prompt, max_tokens=2200, timeout=90):
    api_key = os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "")
    if not api_key:
        return "_[LLM unavailable: missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY]_"

    payload = json.dumps({
        "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
        "max_tokens": max_tokens,
        "system": """You are a Federal Reserve staff economist drafting the inflation outlook section of Tealbook A.

Write in the style of internal FOMC briefing material:
- formal, compact, analytical
- no meta-commentary
- no mention of searching, sources, or the task
- no public-facing explanations
- first sentence must be substantive macroeconomic analysis
- anchor on PCE inflation and distinguish headline from core
- separate current conditions, near-term outlook, and medium-term outlook
- distinguish temporary relative-price movements from underlying inflation pressure

If available information is noisy or conflicting, prioritize official U.S. statistical releases and produce a coherent baseline narrative rather than a news summary.
""",
        "tools": [{"type": "web_search_20250305", "name": "web_search"}],
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}]
    }).encode()

    req = urllib.request.Request(
        "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages",
        data=payload,
        headers={
            "Content-Type": "application/json",
            "x-api-key": api_key,
            "anthropic-version": "2023-06-01"
        }
    )

    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:
            response = json.loads(resp.read())
            blocks = response.get("content", [])
            text = " ".join(
                block["text"] for block in blocks
                if block.get("type") == "text"
            ).strip()
            return text
    except Exception as e:
        return f"_[LLM call failed: {e}]_"


def clean_output_text(text):
    text = text.replace("\r\n", "\n").replace("\r", "\n")
    text = re.sub(r"\s+([,.;:])", r"\1", text)   # remove spaces before punctuation
    text = re.sub(r"[ \t]+", " ", text)          # normalize repeated spaces
    text = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", text)       # collapse excessive blank lines
    return text.strip()


projection_prompt = f"""Today is {_today.strftime("%B %d, %Y")}.

You are preparing the inflation outlook section of Tealbook A for the upcoming FOMC meeting.

Use web search only to retrieve the most recent available U.S. data as of today for:
- headline PCE inflation
- core PCE inflation
- headline CPI inflation
- core CPI inflation
- labor cost growth
- food price developments
- energy price developments
- nonfuel import price developments

Use this source hierarchy:
1. BEA for PCE inflation
2. BLS for CPI, import prices, and labor cost measures
3. EIA for broad energy-price context
4. Highly reliable supplementary sources only if needed

Before writing, determine internally:
1. the latest observed reading for headline PCE
2. the latest observed reading for core PCE
3. the latest observed reading for CPI
4. the latest observed labor-cost measure
5. the main driver of headline inflation
6. the main driver of core inflation
7. the near-term outlook
8. the medium-term outlook
9. the main upside and downside risks
10. the point at which the forecast begins

Do not output that internal checklist. Output only the final narrative and tables.

Substance rules:
- Anchor the narrative on PCE inflation, not CPI
- Use CPI and other indicators only as supporting evidence
- Clearly distinguish observed data from forecast
- Clearly distinguish temporary relative-price movements from underlying inflation pressure
- Do not center the narrative on a geopolitical or commodity-market event unless the data clearly indicate that it is the dominant driver of the inflation outlook
- Do not invent events, policy actions, or data releases
- Do not relabel dates or periods
- Every numerical statement must correspond to the correct release period
- Forecast values must begin only after the last observed release
- The narrative and tables must be mutually consistent

Writing format:
- Exactly 3 paragraphs
- Paragraph 1: current inflation conditions and key drivers
- Paragraph 2: near-term outlook over the next few months
- Paragraph 3: medium-term outlook and main risks

Style rules:
- Begin immediately with substantive economic analysis
- No preamble
- No meta-commentary
- Do not say “Based on my research,” “Here is,” “I will,” or similar phrases
- Third person
- Formal internal staff tone
- Precise, compact, analytical
- No dramatic or journalistic language

After the 3 paragraphs, fill in both tables in markdown.

Table rules:
- One decimal place
- Single estimates only
- Mark with (f) any value that had not yet been released as of today and is therefore a forecast
- Use observed values where available
- Use forecasts only for periods after the latest observed release
- Output complete tables with no commentary before or after them

**TABLE 1 — Annual projections (Percent change at annual rate from final quarter of preceding period):**

| Measure | {_prev_yr} | {_yr} | {_next_yr} | {_far_yr} |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PCE price index | | | | |
| &nbsp;&nbsp;Food and beverages | | | | |
| &nbsp;&nbsp;Energy | | | | |
| &nbsp;&nbsp;Excluding food and energy | | | | |
| &nbsp;&nbsp;Core goods imports* | | | | |

**TABLE 2 — Monthly projections (12-month percent change):**

| Measure | {_m[0]} | {_m[1]} | {_m[2]} | {_f[0]} | {_f[1]} | {_f[2]} |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PCE price index | | | | | | |
| Excluding food and energy | | | | | | |

* Core goods imports exclude computers, semiconductors, oil, and natural gas.
"""

result = call_claude_search(projection_prompt, max_tokens=2400, timeout=90)
result = clean_output_text(result)
display(Markdown(result))
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-inflation-1

# ROW 1 — Chart 1: Headline CPI & PCE  |  Chart 2: Core PCE Measures
fn = next_fig()
fig1 = go.Figure()
for s in recession_shapes(recession, CHART_START):
    fig1.add_shape(**s)
fig1.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=cpi[CHART_START:].index, y=cpi[CHART_START:].values,
    name='CPI', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig1.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=pce[CHART_START:].index, y=pce[CHART_START:].values,
    name='PCE', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig1.add_hline(y=0, line_color='black', line_width=0.8)
layout1 = base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Headline Consumer Price Inflation',
    subtitle='Percent change from year earlier', start=CHART_START)
fig1.update_layout(**layout1)
fig1.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
fig1.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, x_end(cpi)])

fn2 = next_fig()
fig2 = go.Figure()
for s in recession_shapes(recession, CHART_START):
    fig2.add_shape(**s)
fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=trim_pce[CHART_START:].index, y=trim_pce[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Trimmed mean PCE', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=mkt_pce[CHART_START:].index, y=mkt_pce[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Market-based PCE excluding food and energy', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=core_pce[CHART_START:].index, y=core_pce[CHART_START:].values,
    name='PCE excluding food and energy', line=line(C3),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig2.add_hline(y=2.0, line_color=C3, line_dash='dot', line_width=1.0,
    annotation_text='2%', annotation_position='top right',
    annotation_font=dict(size=8, color=C3))
layout2 = base_layout(f'Figure {fn2} — Measures of Underlying PCE Price Inflation',
    subtitle='Percent change from year earlier', start=CHART_START)
fig2.update_layout(**layout2)
fig2.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
fig2.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, x_end(core_pce)])

show(fig1, 'Source: BLS (CPIAUCSL); BEA (PCEPI) via FRED.')
show(fig2, 'Source: Dallas Fed (PCETRIM12M159SFRBDAL); BEA (DPCXRG3M086SBEA, PCEPILFE) via FRED.')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-inflation-2

# ROW 2 — Chart 3: Labor Cost Growth  |  Chart 4: Commodity & Oil Prices
fn3 = next_fig()
fig3 = go.Figure()
for s in recession_shapes(recession, CHART_START):
    fig3.add_shape(**s)
eci_m     = eci.resample('MS').ffill()[CHART_START:]
compnfb_m = compnfb.resample('MS').ffill()[CHART_START:]
fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=eci_m.index, y=eci_m.values,
    name='Employment cost index', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=ahe[CHART_START:].index, y=ahe[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Avg hourly earnings', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=compnfb_m.index, y=compnfb_m.values,
    name='Compensation per hour', line=line(C3),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig3.add_hline(y=0, line_color='black', line_width=0.8)
layout3 = base_layout(f'Figure {fn3} — Labor Cost Growth',
    subtitle='Percent change from year earlier', start=CHART_START)
fig3.update_layout(**layout3)
fig3.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
fig3.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, x_end(ahe)])

fn4 = next_fig()
fig4 = go.Figure()
for s in recession_shapes(recession, CHART_START):
    fig4.add_shape(**s)
fig4.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=brent[CHART_START:].index, y=brent[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Brent crude ($/bbl, left)', yaxis='y1', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: $%{y:.1f}<extra></extra>'))
fig4.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=crb_prx[CHART_START:].index, y=crb_prx[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Global Price Index of All Commodities (index, right)', yaxis='y2', line=line(C3),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}<extra></extra>'))
layout4 = base_layout(f'Figure {fn4} — Commodity and Oil Price Levels',
    subtitle='Dollars per barrel (left); Global Price Index 2016 = 10 (right)',
    start=CHART_START, r_margin=55)
layout4['yaxis']  = dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor='#eeeeee', zeroline=False,
    tickprefix='$', tickfont=dict(size=9), autorange=True, side='left')
layout4['yaxis2'] = dict(overlaying='y', side='right', showgrid=False,
    zeroline=False, tickfont=dict(size=9), autorange=True)
layout4['margin']['r'] = 55
fig4.update_layout(**layout4)
fig4.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, x_end(brent)])

show(fig3, 'Source: BLS (ECIWAG, CES0500000003, COMPNFB) via FRED.')
show(fig4, 'Source: EIA (DCOILBRENTEU); IMF (PALLFNFINDEXM) via FRED.')
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-inflation-3

# ROW 3 — Chart 5: Energy & Import Prices  |  Chart 6: LT Expectations
fn5 = next_fig()
fig5 = go.Figure()
for s in recession_shapes(recession, CHART_START):
    fig5.add_shape(**s)

fig5.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=imp_core[CHART_START:].index, y=imp_core[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Core import prices (left)', yaxis='y1', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))

fig5.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=pce_eng[CHART_START:].index, y=pce_eng[CHART_START:].values,
    name='PCE energy prices (right)', yaxis='y2', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>'))

fig5.add_hline(y=0, line_color='black', line_width=0.8, yref='y')

layout5 = base_layout(f'Figure {fn5} — Energy and Import Price Inflation',
    subtitle='Percent change from year earlier',
    start=CHART_START, r_margin=55)
layout5['yaxis']  = dict(showgrid=True, gridcolor='#eeeeee', zeroline=False,
    ticksuffix='%', tickfont=dict(size=9), autorange=True, side='left')
layout5['yaxis2'] = dict(overlaying='y', side='right', showgrid=False,
    zeroline=False, ticksuffix='%', tickfont=dict(size=9), autorange=True)
layout5['margin']['r'] = 55
fig5.update_layout(**layout5)
fig5.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, x_end(pce_eng)])

fn6 = next_fig()
fig6 = go.Figure()
for s in recession_shapes(recession, CHART_START):
    fig6.add_shape(**s)
fig6.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=tips_fwd[CHART_START:].index, y=tips_fwd[CHART_START:].values,
    name='5-to-10-year-ahead TIPS compensation', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig6.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=clev10[CHART_START:].index, y=clev10[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Cleveland Fed median 10-yr', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig6.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=mich1yr[CHART_START:].index, y=mich1yr[CHART_START:].values,
    name='Michigan median next 12-m', line=line(C3),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig6.add_hline(y=2.0, line_color=C3, line_dash='dot', line_width=1.0,
    annotation_text='2%', annotation_position='bottom right',
    annotation_font=dict(size=8, color=C3))
layout6 = base_layout(f'Figure {fn6} — Long-Term Inflation Expectations',
    subtitle='Percent', start=CHART_START)
fig6.update_layout(**layout6)
fig6.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')
fig6.update_xaxes(range=[CHART_START, x_end(tips_fwd)])

show(fig5, 'Source: BEA (PCEPILFE, IREXFDFLS) via FRED.')
show(fig6, 'Source: FRB (T5YIFRM); Cleveland Fed (EXPINF10YR); U. of Michigan (MICH) via FRED.',
     flush=True)
```

---

## Financial Conditions

```{python}
#| label: fin-data
#| include: false

dgs10  = pull_series('DGS10',        start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
dgs2   = pull_series('DGS2',         start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
hy_spr = pull_series('BAMLH0A0HYM2', start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
ig_spr = pull_series('BAMLC0A4CBBB', start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()
vix    = pull_series('VIXCLS',        start=DATA_START).dropna().resample('MS').mean()

spread_series = (dgs10 - dgs2).dropna()
spread_10_2   = spread_series.iloc[-1]
t10_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(dgs10.index[-1]))
spr_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(hy_spr.index[-1]))
vix_date      = month_label(pd.Timestamp(vix.index[-1]))
```

```{python}
#| label: plot-financial-1

# Row 1: Treasury yields + Credit spreads
fn = next_fig()
fig1 = go.Figure()
fig1.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=dgs10[CHART_START:].index, y=dgs10[CHART_START:].values,
    name='10-yr', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig1.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=dgs2[CHART_START:].index, y=dgs2[CHART_START:].values,
    name='2-yr', line=line(C2),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig1.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=spread_series[CHART_START:].index, y=spread_series[CHART_START:].values,
    name='10s-2s spread', line=line(C3),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.2f}%<extra></extra>'))
fig1.add_hline(y=0, line_width=0.8, line_color='black')
fig1.update_layout(**base_layout(f'Figure {fn} — Treasury Yields & Spread',
    subtitle='Percent, monthly average'))
fig1.update_yaxes(ticksuffix='%')

fn2 = next_fig()
fig2 = go.Figure()
fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=hy_spr[CHART_START:].index, y=hy_spr[CHART_START:].values,
    name='HY (OAS)', line=line(C1),
    hovertemplate='%{x|%b %Y}: %{y:.0f} bp<extra></extra>'))
```
